sections.paragraph:
ἑλκόω (s. ἕλκος; Trag. et al., esp. in medical wr. [Hippocr.: CMG I 1 p. 49, 23; Galen: CMG V 4, 1, 1 p. 80, 13; Hobart 31f; in a letter to a physician: PMerton 12, 20], also Aristeas Hist.: 725 Fgm. 1, 3 Jac [in Eus., PE 9, 25, 3]) cause sores/ulcers pf. pass. ptc. εἱλκωμένος (ἡλ-t.r.; on the reduplication s. B-D-F §68; Rob. 364) covered w. sores (X., De Re Equ. 1, 4; 5, 1; Plut., Phoc. 742 [2, 3]; Artem. 1, 23; 28; 41) Lk 16:20—DELG s.v. ἕλκος.

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

esp. esp. = especially

  1. wr. = writers, writings

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

CMG CMG = Corpus Medicorum Graecorum—List 5

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

Hobart Hobart = WH., Medical Language of St. Luke—List 6

Hist.

Hist. (after a personal name)=historicus, historian

hist. = historian, historical

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

t.r. t.r. = textus receptus

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 318
